name: perl-base
title: perl-base
description:
minimal Perl system
Perl is a scripting language used in many system scripts and utilities.
.
This package provides a Perl interpreter and the small subset of the
standard run-time library required to perform basic tasks. For a full
Perl installation, install "perl" (and its dependencies, "perl-modules-5.40"
and "perl-doc").

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use perl because perl - The Perl 5 language interpreter.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `perl [ -sTtuUWX ]` as documented upstream for perl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `perl                Perl overview (this section)` as documented upstream for perl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `perlintro           Perl introduction for beginners` as documented upstream for perl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `perlrun             Perl execution and options` as documented upstream for perl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `perl [ -sTtuUWX ]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: perl - The Perl 5 language interpreter.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: perl - The Perl 5 language interpreter.
